title: docker --mount type=volume (named volume, not bind) resolves SAFE (ADR 0012 type-skip)

origin.description: Hand-written contract seed locking the ADR 0012 extractor's `type !== "bind"` skip: a --mount with type=volume has no host path, so even though it carries src=appdata it must NOT be flagged. No existing case covers the type=volume branch.

humanExpectation.rationale: A --mount of type=volume names a docker named volume (src=appdata), not a host path. The ADR 0012 host-path extractor only reads type=bind mounts (the type !== 'bind' branch continues), so files.broad-path does NOT fire. Pinned image, no env, no other surface → SAFE. Locks that the extractor does not over-flag volume mounts.
knownLimitations: Synthetic minimal shape; src=appdata is a named volume, not a path.
